title: bidens sandvicensis subsp. confusa
intro: Bidens sandvicensis subsp. confusa, also known as the confused beggar-ticks, is a subspecies of the Bidens sandvicensis plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The health benefits of Bidens sandvicensis subsp. confusa include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to protect against cancer and other diseases. Bidens sandvicensis subsp. confusa can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
